candidates.evidence_quote: This decrease was primarily due to menu price increases in excess of wage rate inflation (0.6%)
could lead to further wage inflation, product and services cost inflation, disruptions in the supply chain, staffing challenges
Adverse weather conditions and natural disasters may further exacerbate a number of these factors.
Our revenue growth is primarily driven by new restaurant openings and increases in comparable restaurant sales.
primarily due to additional revenue related to new restaurant openings and an increase in comparable restaurant sales
based on an increase of 4.7% in menu pricing, partially offset by a 3.0% negative change from menu mix
a 3.0% negative change from menu mix
The increase from fiscal 2023 was primarily driven by an increase in average check of 1.7%
partially offset by decreased customer traffic of 0.7%
Sales through the off-premise channel comprised approximately 21% of our restaurant sales during fiscal 2024
North Italia comparable sales increased approximately 2% from fiscal 2023.
Flower Child comparable sales increased approximately 6% from fiscal 2023.
Total operating weeks at The Cheesecake Factory restaurants increased 1.9% to 11,214 in fiscal 2024
Margins are subject to fluctuations in commodity costs, labor, restaurant-level occupancy expenses
food and beverage costs were 22.5% for fiscal 2024 compared to 23.4% for fiscal 2023, due primarily to menu price increases in excess of inflation across most categories
labor expenses, which include restaurant-level labor costs and bakery production labor, including associated fringe benefits, were 35.3% and 35.7%
partially offset by higher management salaries due to improved staffing levels
occupancy expenses (rent, common area expenses, insurance, licenses, taxes and utilities)
As a percentage of revenues, G&A expenses were 6.4% and 6.3% for fiscal 2024 and fiscal 2023, respectively.
Preopening costs can fluctuate significantly from period to period based on the number, mix and timing of restaurant openings
we recorded impairment of assets and lease terminations expense of $13.6 million primarily related to impairment of long-lived assets
partially offset by lease termination income, net for four The Cheesecake Factory restaurants
the fair value of the contingent consideration and compensation liability decreased by $5.3 million to $20.2 million
Goodwill and other indefinite-lived intangible assets are not amortized and are tested for impairment annually
we had an income tax provision of $14.3 million, an effective tax rate of 8.3%
a higher amount of employment tax credits (1.5%)
Cash flows from operations increased by $49.9 million from fiscal 2023 primarily due to higher net income
We anticipate approximately $190 million to $210 million in capital expenditures to support this level of unit development
We repurchased 0.5 million shares at a cost of $18.0 million, excluding excise tax, during fiscal 2024
Common stock dividends of $53.0 million and $53.2 million were paid in fiscal 2024 and 2023, respectively.
In the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024, we repaid $20.0 million on the Revolver Facility.
we are subject to financial covenants, as well as to customary events of default that, if triggered, could result in acceleration of the maturity of the Revolver Facility
which will mature on June 15, 2026, unless earlier repurchased, redeemed or converted
leveraging incremental sales to increase restaurant-level margins at The Cheesecake Factory concept
focused primarily on menu innovation, service and operational execution to differentiate ourselves from other restaurant concepts
partnering with a third party to provide delivery services for our restaurants
produce quality cheesecakes and other baked products for our restaurants, international licensees and third-party bakery customers
leveraging our bakery operations, international and consumer packaged goods royalty revenue streams

notes: Chunk = 1 event only: 10-K 0001410578-25-000195 (2025-02-24), part 5/6 = MD&A (results of operations, liquidity, critical accounting estimates). No fiscal_kpis in this chunk.
All 38 evidence quotes verified as exact substrings of the event content.

Skipped (vague/banned, 11): shifts in consumer behavior (vague); geopolitical and macroeconomic events (bare category); natural disasters (no restaurant-specific mechanism beyond weather sentence); gift card program and Cheesecake Rewards marketing items (strategy laundry-list; Cheesecake brand = company legal name, banned); Other FRC segment sales (FRC = acquired subsidiary company name, banned in name); interest expense (table-only, no narrative attribution); stock-based compensation (mechanical non-GAAP addback); consumer packaged goods royalties (single list mention, thin); total-return/financial-guidance target (no specific metric); 7% unit growth target ('growth' banned; cause covered by new_restaurant_development).

Cheesecake Factory brand-level comps NOT coined separately because the brand name equals the company legal name (banned); company-wide same_store_sales covers it. North Italia and Flower Child brand comps coined per R9 (brand metric = own driver).
new_restaurant_development named without banned state word 'openings'; evidence sentence explicitly attributes revenue growth to new restaurant openings.
